expectations.text: Response uses pgTable for table definitions
expectations.evidence: All three tables use pgTable: "export const organizations = pgTable('organizations', {...})", "export const users = pgTable('users', {...})", "export const invoices = pgTable('invoices', {...})".

expectations.text: Response creates pgEnum for status and role columns
expectations.evidence: Two pgEnums defined: "export const roleEnum = pgEnum('role', ['owner', 'admin', 'member']);" and "export const invoiceStatusEnum = pgEnum('invoice_status', ['draft', 'sent', 'paid', 'overdue']);". Note: organizations.plan uses text('plan') instead of a pgEnum, but role and status columns do use pgEnum.

expectations.text: Response defines relations() for all table relationships
expectations.evidence: Relations defined for all tables: organizationsRelations (many users, many invoices), usersRelations (one organization), invoicesRelations (one organization, one creator). However, usersRelations is missing the many(invoices) side for createdInvoices.

expectations.text: Response includes indexes on org_id columns
Response creates uniqueIndex on (org_id, number) for invoices
